task_completion.description: Verify that the FZK-Haus model is loaded in the Bonsai (BlenderBIM) environment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender interface should be open, displaying the 3D residential building model (FZK-Haus) in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two smoke detectors (IfcAlarm) have been added to the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Blender Outliner (scene tree) or 3D viewport for at least two newly created objects designated as 'IfcAlarm' or smoke detectors.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two fire extinguishers (IfcFireSuppressionTerminal) have been added to the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Blender Outliner or 3D viewport for at least two newly created objects designated as 'IfcFireSuppressionTerminal' or fire extinguishers.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct IFC PredefinedTypes are assigned to the life safety equipment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Object Properties or IFC Object panel in Blender. You should see the PredefinedType 'SMOKE' for the IfcAlarm objects and 'FIREEXTINGUISHER' for the IfcFireSuppressionTerminal objects.

task_completion.description: Verify that the newly created life safety elements are spatially contained within a building storey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Outliner's spatial tree hierarchy. The IfcAlarm and IfcFireSuppressionTerminal objects must be nested underneath a building storey node (e.g., 'Ground Floor', 'First Floor', or an 'IfcBuildingStorey' element).

task_completion.description: Verify that the updated project is saved to the specified output file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog, a Blender info notification at the bottom of the screen, or a file manager window showing 'fzk_fire_equipment.ifc' being saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the Blender/Bonsai GUI or its built-in scripting tools to modify the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Blender interface being actively used (menus opened, outliner modified, or Blender's text editor used), rather than just a terminal window.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the BIM software by manually typing IFC entities into a raw text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be using a plain text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to manually insert raw IFC string lines into the .ifc file.

integrity.description: Ensure the elements added are genuine IFC objects within the BIM environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The added elements should appear in the Blender outliner with proper IFC icons or properties, not just as renamed empty text objects or fake nodes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires adding at least 2 IfcAlarm objects (PredefinedType: SMOKE) and at least 2 IfcFireSuppressionTerminal objects (PredefinedType: FIREEXTINGUISHER). The elements must be spatially contained within a building storey in the Bonsai/BlenderBIM outliner. The final output must be saved exactly to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_fire_equipment.ifc. The specification document is located at /home/ga/Desktop/fire_safety_brief.txt.
